ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Architectural Illustrators create detailed drawings and visualizations of buildings and structures for architects, engineers, and clients. They must have a keen eye for detail and be skilled in various software and techniques.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Architectural Illustrators work closely with architects, engineers, and clients to create accurate and visually appealing drawings and visualizations of buildings and structures. They must have a strong understanding of architectural principles, building codes, and design software. They must also be skilled in various techniques, such as hand drawing, computer-aided design (CAD), and 3D modeling.

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in art and design can pursue a career as an Architectural Illustrator.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree in art, design, or a related field can transition into an Architectural Illustrator role with additional training and certifications.

future_builder.description: Experienced Architectural Illustrators can advance to senior roles or start their own businesses with additional skills and certifications.

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into an Architectural Illustrator role with relevant skills and a strong portfolio.
survival_skills_needed: Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, etc.)
Basic understanding of architectural principles and building codes
